ClinicalDocument.title: Exit from Mental Health and Substance Misuse Specialist Services

section2.text: Tony needs his hearing aid to hear clearly - a visit from an Audiologist has provided Tony with a new rechargeable hearing aid - although he does still need support to ensure he keeps it recharged and cleaned. Native Language Irish.
section2.title: Communication and Comprehension

section2.text: Tony has been found a bedsit flat in the Uptown Housing Association development on Smith Street. Living on own. Private rented.
section2.title: Housing

section3.text: You will need to use the intercom to gain access.
section3.title: Property Access Information

section2.text:
Tony has been attending the Church of the Virgin Mary, Uptown some Sunday mornings.
Ethnicity: White Irish.
Religion Philosophy: Roman Catholic Church.
section2.title: Ethnicity, Religion, Philosophy & Culture

section2.text: A supervisory role has been found for Tony within a sheltered work environment.
section2.title: Education, Training, Employment and Financial Status

section3.text: Tony is currently in receipt of Incapacity Benefit since 02/04/2004, which maybe withdrawn once he commences the employment found for him.
section3.title: Financial Status

COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.title: Referral, First Contact, Triage and Entry

section3.text: Tony was expressing some grandiose ideas about who he is that lead staff at the hostel to become concerned for him. He can be aggressive and personally invasive, however he had not been observed being physically violent.
section3.title: Reason for Referral / First Contact

section2.text: Date Recorded: 01/01/2004. URGENT. The referral indicates that it is important that Tony is seen urgently.
section2.title: Triage Decision

section3.text: Tony was expressing grandiose ideas and was aggressive and invasive.
section3.title: Presenting Problems

section3.text: Tony's symptoms indicate hypomania. Difficulties with accommodation were also identified.
section3.title: Outcome of Specialist Service Assessment

section2.title: Further Details or Concerns Relating to Current Contact Status

COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.title: Care Teams or Organisations Involved in Care Delivery

section2.text:
Status: Organisation with MHA Section 117 responsibility.
Date when the first entry for this team was recorded: 04/05/2004
section2.title: Main Care Team

section3.title: Involvement in Patient Care or Support

tr.td: Care Coordinator/Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker at Uptown Social Services

COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.title: Persons with a Connection to the Care of Patient

section2.text: Tony has a hearing problem for which he has seen an Audiologist and has been fitted with appropriate new hearing aids.
section2.title: Registered Disability / Other Condition(s)

section2.text:
Primary Substance: Alcohol.
Age of first use = 12.
Taken Orally
section2.title: Problem Substances Information

section3.text: He was detained under MHA Section 2 on 01/01/2004, with symptoms of Hypomania. This was converted to a section 3 on 26/01/2004. Tony was removed from Section on 09/09/04.
section3.title: Mental Health Act History

section2.text: CPA has ended and there are no continuing care plan components at this time.
section2.title: Care Plan Summary

section2.text: If Tony deteriorates the Uptown CMHT should be contacted to see if further care is needed. This will have to be treated as a new care spell and an MHA Assessment will need doing - if not done already.
section2.title: Contingency Plan

section2.text:
Date of Risk Assessment: 01/01/2004.
Risk Assessment Tool: Core Face
section2.title: Risk Assessment

section3.text:
Category: Violence to Adults.
Scale: MEDIUM.
Tony becomes verbally abusive when he doesn't get his way. Although he has not actually been physically violent he can be extremely intimidating.
section3.title: Risk Assessment Categories and Scale Scores

section4.text:
Category: H1 Previous Violence
Scale: 1
Indications have recently come to our attention that in his younger life Tony was convicted of Breach of the Peace connected to criminal activities - however there has not been any recent evidence of violent behaviours
section4.title: HCR20 Outcome Score

section2.text:
Risk Factor: When he has had a considerable amount of alcohol he can be verbally aggressive.
Impact: Increases Risk
section2.title: Factors Increasing or Decreasing Risk

section2.text:
Date: 01/05/2002.
Category: Aggression to Adults.
Level: High.
Tony has a long history of aggressive behaviour which whilst it has not seemed to extend into physical violence. He was arrested for a breach of the peace in May 2002 and was bound over to keep the peace at that time
section2.title: Risk Activities History

section2.text: Item 3: Alcohol/Drug Problem = 3 - moderately severe problem
section2.title: HoNOS Tool Outcome Scores

COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.text: No formal mental health history previously This care episode commenced on 01/01/04, with Tony admitted with indications of Hypomania. After an 8 month admission he was discharged to live independently in the community with support from the CMHT. He has good insight and is willing to engage with professionals.
COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.title: Additional Information

code.displayName: Mental Health Triage Decision Identifies Need as Urgent
Proxy Care Coordinator/Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker
Care Process Date Commenced or First Review
Care Process Date Concluded or Most Recent Review
